LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CHRISTISON COMPANY INC. & THE GLASSWORKS II HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF, AS AN EMPLOYEE OF A SUBCONTRACTOR, CANNOT ESTABLISH THAT DEFENDANT OWED HIM A DUTY OF CARE AS A MATTER OF LAW UNDER PRIVETTE V. SUP. CRT. (1993) 5 CAL.4TH 689 AND SEABRIGHT INS. CO. V. U.S. AIRWAYS (2011) 52 CAL.4TH 590. THERE IS NO EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT NEGLIGENTLY RETAINED CONTROL OF THE WORK OR ENGAGED IN ANY OTHER NEGLIGENCE THAT AFFIRMATIVELY CONTRIBUTED TO THE INJURY. UNDER SEABRIGHT, DEFENDANT DELEGATED ANY OBLIGATIONS IT HAD UNDER THE OSHA REGULATIONS TO PLAINTIFF'S EMPLOYER. THERE IS ALSO NO EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT CONCEALED A DEFECT ON THE PROPERTY THAT CONTRIBUTED TO THE INJURY THAT PLAINTIFF'S EMPLOYER COULD NOT DISCOVER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

PLAINTIFF ALVARO MIGUEL's Motion For Reconsideration Of The Court'S Order On Motion For Summary Judgment is transferred to be heard in Department 504 before Judge Busch. The hearing will occur on April 19, 2013, at 2:00 p.m. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: JORDAN SUSSMAN, ESQ., 415.986.3644, FOR PLAINTIFF ALVARO SAUL MIGUEL.
JOHN HOURIHAN, ESQ., 510.457.3455, FOR DEFENDANTS CHRISTISON COMPANY INC. AND THE GLASSWORKS II HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION.
RICHARD MORTON, ESQ., 714.426.4603, FOR DEFENDANT PREFERRED BUILDING MAINTENANCE, INC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Defendants Christison Company Inc. & The Glassworks II Homeowners Association's Motion for Summary Judgment is granted. Plaintiff, as an employee of a subcontractor, cannot establish that defendant owed him a duty of care as a matter of law under Privette v. Sup. Crt. (1993) 5 Cal.4th 689 and Seabright Ins. Co. v. U.S. Airways (2011) 52 Cal.4th 590. There is no evidence that defendant negligently retained control of the work or engaged in any other negligence that affirmatively contributed to the injury. Under Seabright, defendant delegated any obligations it had under the OSHA regulations to plaintiff's employer. There is also no evidence that defendant concealed a defect on the property that contributed to the injury that plaintiff's employer could not discover. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
